title: Ukraine Destroys 350th Russian Helicopter — Ka-52 Shot Down by FPV Drone
detail: Ukrainian drone operators destroyed a Russian Ka-52 'Alligator' attack helicopter using an FPV drone in Donetsk Oblast on March 20, marking the 350th Russian helicopter shot down by Ukraine since the start of the full-scale invasion. The Ka-52 is Russia's most advanced attack helicopter, with an estimated value of $16M+. Ukrainian forces also downed a second military helicopter the following day, continuing a streak of successful anti-helicopter operations.

title: Ukrainian Long-Range Drones Damage Russia's Beriev A-50 AWACS Aircraft at Novgorod Repair Plant
detail: Ukrainian drones struck a repair plant in Russia's Novgorod region on March 20, damaging a Beriev A-50 airborne early warning and control (AWACS) aircraft valued at approximately $500 million. The A-50 is Russia's primary airborne radar platform, critical for coordinating fighter aircraft and tracking Ukrainian air defenses. The strike extended Ukraine's long-range drone campaign into the Novgorod region, approximately 500 km northwest of Kyiv. Russia scrambled interceptors but was unable to prevent the strike.

title: Russian Central Bank Cuts Interest Rate to 15% — Seventh Cut Since June 2025
detail: The Bank of Russia cut its key interest rate from 15.5% to 15% on March 20, 2026 — the seventh consecutive cut since June 2025, when the rate peaked at 21%. The decision reflects slowing Russian inflation (to approximately 5.9%) and a cooling wartime economy as oil export revenues come under sustained pressure from Ukrainian drone strikes on Baltic ports, Western tanker seizures, and the ongoing G7 price cap. The EU simultaneously extended restrictive measures against ~2,600 individuals and entities responsible for undermining Ukraine's territorial integrity for another six months, until September 15, 2026.
